title: Update CentOS 8 packages
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug 

**Description**
Dev releases for CentOS `podman` at https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/ is no longer updated, last version build was `3.2.3`

currnet release version is `3.4.x`, please update buids to newer version

comments.body:
Stream builds available in http://mirror.centos.org/centos/8-stream/AppStream/x86_64/os/Packages/

Is `RHEL/CentOS` renames `3.4.0` as `4.0.0`?
No, that's the current upstream development version - 4.0 will be released in January.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lsm5 is it possible to setup some GH workflow actions to update sources. Now OBS build service supports GitHub integraions. Give me some guidelines, I would be happy help/maintain

@srbala we recently removed installation steps for Kubic packages on CentOS 8 from the official podman docs because the Kubic packages were causing issues for someone (I think it was the Ceph team). Given that removal + CentOS 8 going EOL sometime this December (IIRC) + availability of latest podman builds in in the default repos on CentOS 8 Stream (& 9 Stream), it's really not worth maintaining the Kubic CentOS packages.

HTH.
